journalName: RESEARCH ON LANGUAGE AND SOCIAL INTERACTION
authors: Esther González-Martínez Angeliki Balantani Department of Social Sciences
raw_title: The “I have (someone)” method: Involving a hospital coworker in the matter at hand
title_en: The “I have (someone)” method: Involving a hospital coworker in the matter at hand
title_zh: The “I have (someone)” method: Involving a hospital coworker in the matter at hand
plain_summary_en: This paper studies The “I have (someone)” method: Involving a hospital coworker in the matter at hand. In plain terms, it asks what is happening, why it matters for public communication, and what evidence the authors use. The likely focus is The, Have, Someone, Method. The abstract suggests a mixed methods approach, which makes the work useful for understanding how communication changes in everyday life.
plain_summary_zh: This paper studies The “I have (someone)” method: Involving a hospital coworker in the matter at hand. In plain terms, it asks what is happening, why it matters for public communication, and what evidence the authors use. The likely focus is The, Have, Someone, Method. The abstract suggests a mixed methods approach, which makes the work useful for understanding how communication changes in everyday life.
research_direction_en: The paper points to an active research direction around The, Have, Someone, Method, especially how institutions, platforms, and audiences shape communication outcomes.
research_direction_zh: The paper points to an active research direction around The, Have, Someone, Method, especially how institutions, platforms, and audiences shape communication outcomes.
